prompt:
this concourse job is taking ~9 minutes and most of that is the first stage. heres the relevant slice from our pipeline.yml:

```yaml
jobs:
  - name: build-and-publish
    plan:
      - get: app-src
        trigger: true
      - get: ci
      - get: golang-builder
      - get: deploy-keys
      - get: chart-repo
      - get: version
      - task: unit
        file: ci/tasks/unit/task.yml
      - task: build
        file: ci/tasks/build/task.yml
      - put: app-image
        params: { image: build/image.tar }
```

basic gut says theres no reason all those gets need to run sequentially but i don't want to break the trigger behavior or end up paying for 30 cold workers. how do we speed this up? assume the worker pool is healthy and we have 4-6 concourse workers normally available.

assertions.name: wraps gets in in_parallel
assertions.description: Output YAML wraps the 6 gets in an `in_parallel:` block

assertions.name: preserves trigger on app-src
assertions.description: `trigger: true` is preserved on the `app-src` get

assertions.name: recommends fail_fast
assertions.description: Output includes `fail_fast: true` on the in_parallel block, with a brief reason

assertions.name: recommends limit
assertions.description: Output includes a `limit:` (4-6 is reasonable given 4-6 workers) so worker pool isn't blown out

assertions.name: task and put remain sequential after gets
assertions.description: The `task: unit`, `task: build`, and `put: app-image` steps stay sequential after the gets — they aren't moved into the in_parallel block

assertions.name: explains why it's safe
assertions.description: Response briefly explains why parallelizing gets is safe (gets are independent, no shared state, plan still waits for slowest get before running tasks)
